key_insight: A single rollout can provide token-level learning signals by training a critic to estimate Pass@k success probability from prefix states, reducing expensive multi-sample credit assignment.
engineering_relevance: This offers a more sample-efficient RL training strategy for language models, especially when heterogeneous reasoning traces make standard advantage estimation unstable.
method: Use one rollout per prompt instead of repeated contrastive sampling.
Train a calibrated critic on Monte Carlo outcomes to predict Pass@k at each prefix.
As k grows, interpret Pass@k as a reachability-style signal for whether a prefix can still lead to success.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports stable learning dynamics and consistent gains in Pass@128 success on mathematical reasoning benchmarks including HMMT26 and AIME24.
watch_item: The approach depends on critic calibration and the quality of Monte Carlo estimates from limited rollouts, which may be sensitive in harder or noisier tasks.
paper_title: Learning with a Single Rollout via Monte Carlo Pass@k Critic
